source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/UnivariateAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- A10 LOOSE smoke oracle. UnivariateAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over a Normal model, then assembles the frequency (mode) curve + credible band on the exceedance grid. The seeded chain reproduces bit-identically across C++/R/Python (identical compiled core + bit-exact Mersenne Twister; see fixtures/estimation/bayes_normal.json's short-run reproducibility note). This case deliberately uses a SHORT run (iterations 100, output_length 400) to stay before the population-sampler output-phase divergence. Values are SELF-COMPUTED from the C++ runner at LOOSE rel tol; A11's emitter may tighten them against the real C# UnivariateAnalysis. The exceedance grid ascends, so the mode (quantile) curve descends (asserted structurally via the two mode_curve rows).
